title: Performance Review Process and Self-Assessment Tips
text:
Q: What is the performance review process, timeline, and how can I effectively complete my self-assessment?
A: The performance review process occurs annually, typically starting in January and concluding by the end of March. Employees will receive a notification in early January to begin their self-assessment, which is due by January 31st. After self-assessments are submitted, managers will review them and schedule one-on-one meetings to discuss performance, set goals, and provide feedback during February. Final reviews and any necessary adjustments to performance ratings will be completed by the end of March. For an effective self-assessment, reflect on your accomplishments, challenges, and areas for improvement over the past year. Use specific examples and align your achievements with company goals. If you have questions or need assistance, please reach out to your HR representative or your direct supervisor before the self-assessment deadline.
